summary: DHH shares a brief link to Jakob Nielsen's article about avoiding commodity status in the PC market through better usability, hardware quality, and support. He enthusiastically endorses Nielsen's argument. He adds a personal note about frustrating experiences with Sony and IBM, expressing a willingness to pay for companies that deliver excellent service and earn customer loyalty.
key_points: Endorses Jakob Nielsen's view that PCs can differentiate through usability, quality hardware, and support rather than competing purely on price
Personal frustrations with Sony and IBM highlight the gap between what customers want and what they receive
Willing customers exist who will reward companies that prioritize service and experience
money_quotes: My experiences with Sony and IBM the last couple of weeks have left me craving for companies to love.
I have money to spend. Serve me well and I will love you.
key_insight: Customers are willing to pay a premium and give loyalty to companies that differentiate through superior usability and service rather than competing on price alone.
